pages.title: HELPFUL HINTS

spokenBlocks.displayText: In some cases you may find a dagesh (dot) in letters other than ב כ פ ת. With these exceptions, the dagesh is a matter of grammar and does not affect the pronunciation of letters.
spokenBlocks.summaryText: In some cases you may find a dagesh (dot) in letters other than

spokenBlocks.displayText: In some limited cases a Kamatz ( ָ )  is pronounced “aw” as in the word "aught" rather than "ah." In this case the Kamatz is called a Kamatz Katan. (There is also a Chataf Kamatz ֳ which sounds like "aw" as well as "oh.") For instance, the Hebrew word for "all," כָּל‚ is pronounced "kol" not "kal." וֹי sounds like "oy." וּי sounds like "oo-ee." אֱלֹהֶיךָ = אֱלֹהֶךָ The י is not pronounced after a ֶ If there is a dagesh (dot) in a Final Chaf,ךָּ, it is pronounced like a כּ as in the word וִיחֻנֶךָּ ,“vichuneka.” If there is a dagesh (dot) in a ה at the end of a word, it is called a "mapik." In these cases, the "h" sound is pronounced.

spokenBlocks.displayText: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה, יְיָ אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ, מֶֽלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם, אֲשֶׁר קִדְּשָֽׁנוּ בְּמִצְוֹתָיו וְרָֽצָה בָֽנוּ, וְשַׁבַּת קָדְשׁוֹ בְּאַהֲבָה וּבְרָצוֹן הִנְחִילָֽנוּ, זִכָּרוֹן לְמַעֲשֵׂה בְרֵאשִׁית. כִּי הוא יוֹם תְּחִלָּה לְמִקְרָאֵי קֹֽדֶשׁ, זֵֽכֶר לִיצִיאַת מִצְרָֽיִם.

spokenBlocks.displayText: Blessed is Adonai our God, Ruler of the universe, who hallows us with mitzvot and takes delight in us. In love and favor God has made the holy Sabbath our heritage, as a reminder of the work of creation. It is first among our sacred days, and a remembrance of the Exodus from Egypt.

spokenBlocks.displayText: O God, You have chosen us and set us apart from all the peoples, and in love and favor have given us the Sabbath day as a sacred inheritance.

spokenBlocks.displayText: Blessed is Adonai our God, Ruler of the universe, Rock of all creation, Righteous One of all generations, the faithful God whose word is deed, whose every command is just and true.
spokenBlocks.summaryText: Blessed is Adonai our God, Ruler of the universe, Rock of all creation,

spokenBlocks.displayText: עַל־הַתּוֹרָה וְעַל־הַעֲבוֹדָה וְעַל־הַנְבִיאִים וְעַל־יוֹם הַשַׁבָּת הַזֶּה, שֶׁנָּתַֽתָּ־לָֽנוּ, יְיָ אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ, לִקְדֻשָּׁה וְלִמְנוּחָה, לְכָבוֹד וּלְתִפְאֶֽרֶת, עַל־הַכֹּל, יְיָ אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ, אֲנַֽחְנוּ מוֹדִים לָךְ, וּמְבָרְכִים אוֹתָךְ. יִתְבָּרַךְ שִׁמְךָ בְּפִי כָּל־חַי תָּמִיד

spokenBlocks.displayText: For the Torah, for the privilege of worship, for the prophets, and for the Shabbat that You, Adonai our God, have given us for holiness and rest, for honor and glory, we thank and bless You. May Your name be blessed for ever by every living being.

spokenBlocks.displayText: מַה נִשְׁתַּנָּה הַלַּֽיְלָה הַזֶּה מִכָּל הַלֵּילוֹת?
spokenBlocks.summaryText: מַה נִשְׁתַּנָּה הַלַּֽיְלָה הַזֶּה מִכָּל הַלֵּילוֹת?

spokenBlocks.displayText: שֶׁבְּכָל הַלֵּילוֹת אָֽנוּ אוֹכְלִין חָמֵץ וּמַצָה, הַלַּֽיְלָה הַזֶּה כֻּלּוֹ מַצָּה.
spokenBlocks.summaryText: שֶׁבְּכָל הַלֵּילוֹת אָֽנוּ אוֹכְלִין חָמֵץ וּמַצָה, הַלַּֽיְלָה הַזֶּה כֻּלּוֹ מַצָּה.

spokenBlocks.displayText: שֶׁבְּכָל הַלֵּילוֹת אָֽנוּ אוֹכְלִין שְׁאָר יְרָקוֹת, הַלַּֽיְלָה הַזֶּה מָרוֹר.
spokenBlocks.summaryText: שֶׁבְּכָל הַלֵּילוֹת אָֽנוּ אוֹכְלִין שְׁאָר יְרָקוֹת, הַלַּֽיְלָה הַזֶּה מָרוֹר.

spokenBlocks.displayText: Why is this night different from all other nights?
spokenBlocks.summaryText: Why is this night different from all other nights?

spokenBlocks.displayText: On all other nights, we eat either levened bread or matzah; on this night-only matzah.
spokenBlocks.summaryText: On all other nights, we eat either levened bread or matzah; on this

spokenBlocks.displayText: On all other nights, we eat all kinds of herbs; on this night, we espe- cially eat bitter herbs.

spokenBlocks.displayText: Hear O Israel: Adonai is our God, Adonai is !שְׁמַע יִשְׂרָאֵל: יְיָ אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ, יְיָ אֶחָד Blessed is God's glorious kingdom for ever and ever! בָּרוּךְ שֵׁם כְּבוֹד מַלְכוּתוֹ לְעוֹלָם וָעֶד!
spokenBlocks.summaryText: Hear O Israel: Adonai is our God, Adonai is !שְׁמַע יִשְׂרָאֵל: יְיָ אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ, יְיָ אֶחָד

spokenBlocks.displayText: וְאָהַבְתָּ אֵת יְיָ אֱלֹהֶֽיךָ בְּכָל־לְבָבְךָ וּבְכָל־נַפְשְׁךָ וּבְכָל־מְאֹדֶֽךָ. וְהָיוּ הַדְּבָרִים הָאֵֽלֶּה, אֲשֶׁר אָנֹכִי מְצַוְּךָ הַיּוֹם, עַל־לְבָבֶֽךָ. וְשִׁנַּנְתָּם לְבָנֶֽיךָ, וְדִבַּרְתָּ בָּם בְּשִׁבְתְּךָ בְּבֵיתֶֽךָ, וּבְלֶכְתְּךָ בַדֶּֽרֶךְ, וּבְשָׁכְבְּךָ וּבְקוּמֶֽךָ.

spokenBlocks.displayText: The people of Israel shall keep the Sabbath, observing the Sabbath in every generation as a covenant for all time. It is a sign for ever between Me and the people of Israel, for in six days the Eternal God made heaven and earth, and on the seventh day God rested from [His] labors.

spokenBlocks.displayText: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה, יְיָ אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ וֵאלֹהֵי אֲבוֹתֵֽינוּ, אֱלֹהֵי אַבְרָהָם, אֱלֹהֵי יִצְחָק, וֵאלֹהֵי יַעֲקֹב: הָאֵל הַגָּדוֹל, הַגִּבּוֹר וְהַנּוֹרָא, אֵל עֶלְיוֹן. גּוֹמֶל חֲסָדִים טוֹבִים, וְקוֹנֵה הַכֹּל, וְזוֹכֵר חַסְדֵי אָבוֹת, וּמֵבִיא גְאֻלָּה לִבְנֵי בְנֵיהֶם, לְמַֽעַן שְׁמוֹ, בְּאַהֲבָה.

Sovereign of all the living, Your ways are the ways of love. You remember the faithfulness of our ancestors, and in love bring redemption to their children's children for the sake of Your name.
